windows.embed_text_v2: beekeeper pulling capped honeycomb frame from beehive box with hive tool, inspecting wax comb, white bee suit and gloves, outdoor apiary, honey harvesting (not candle making)
windows.summary_v2: A veiled beekeeper pulls a capped honeycomb frame out of a beehive and holds it up to inspect it outdoors.

seconds_v2.desc: Open beehive box seen from above; rows of wax-capped honeycomb frames, a gloved hand reaching in at right holding a hive tool.
Gloved beekeeper's hand grips a frame inside the hive, honey-glazed comb and propolis-stained wooden frames filling the box.
Beekeeper continues loosening a honeycomb frame within the hive box, sticky capped comb visible across the frame tops.
Beekeeper lifts a honeycomb frame upward out of the hive, exposing the parallel wooden frame slots below.
A pulled honeycomb frame is raised overhead, backlit, showing dense capped cells; empty hive box below.
Close view of the raised honeycomb frame, mottled white and amber capped cells, outdoor bare branches behind.
Bearded beekeeper in a white veiled suit holds the full honeycomb frame up with both gloved hands, inspecting it outdoors.
Beekeeper holds the frame and a hive tool, face visible through the veil; 'FLOW' logo on the suit; sun flare in frame.
Beekeeper turns the honeycomb frame, scraping/holding it with the hive tool, examining the capped comb against bare trees.

windows.embed_text: a bowl filled with food sitting on top of a table | a pot filled with food sitting on top of a stove | a pot of food on a stove with a pot of food in it
windows.embed_text_v2: melting raw honeycomb in metal bowl over hot water bath double boiler on stove, rendering beeswax and honey, stainless pot, gas range, honey harvesting (not candle making)
windows.summary_v2: Raw honeycomb is melted down in a water-bath / double boiler on a stove to render wax and honey, in a honey-harvesting context.

seconds_v2.desc: Chunks of raw white honeycomb piled in a metal bowl set inside a larger enamel pot of water on a stovetop, steam rising.
Wider view: stainless bowl of honeycomb nested in a yellow-handled pot on a gas range, cast-iron pans beside it.
Honeycomb chunks beginning to soften in the hot-water bath, comb structure still intact in the metal bowl.
Honeycomb continues to melt down in the simmering double-boiler setup, wax and honey loosening from the cells.
Comb collapsing further into the heated bowl, cell walls slumping as wax warms over the water bath.
Honeycomb mostly broken down into a slushy mass of wax and honey in the bowl over simmering water.
Melting comb now a frothy pale mixture; steam visible around the bowl on the stove.
Steam fogs the frame as the honeycomb mixture melts down further in the metal bowl over the pot.
A second large stockpot of pale frothy melted comb/honey simmers in front; a person in jeans stands at left pouring from the first pot.
